questions.question: Explain the primary function of the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) within a CPU, and list two specific operations it can perform.
questions.modelAnswer: The primary function of the ALU is to perform arithmetic and logical operations on data. Arithmetic operations include addition and subtraction. Logical operations include AND, OR, and NOT.

questions.question: Describe the roles of the Program Counter (PC) and the Memory Address Register (MAR) during the instruction fetch phase of the fetch-decode-execute cycle.
questions.modelAnswer: The Program Counter (PC) holds the memory address of the next instruction to be fetched. This address is copied from the PC to the Memory Address Register (MAR). The MAR then supplies this address to the memory unit, which uses it to retrieve the instruction. After the fetch, the PC is incremented to point to the next sequential instruction.

questions.question: Trace the journey of a single instruction through the major functional units of a CPU during the fetch, decode, and execute cycles. Your answer should detail the role of the PC, MAR, MDR, IR, Control Unit, and ALU.
questions.modelAnswer: Fetch: The PC holds the instruction address, copied to the MAR. Memory returns the instruction code to the MDR, which loads the IR. The PC increments. Decode: The Control Unit interprets the opcode in the IR and activates the necessary control signals for the execute phase. Execute: The Control Unit directs data flow. Operands may be fetched from registers. The ALU performs the operation (e.g., addition). Results are written back to a register or memory via the MDR. The cycle then repeats with the next instruction from the PC.
